focus: cross-dimension stabilization and adversarial replay of all five authoritative P1 claims

findingDetails.title: Completed research phases retain pre-run continuity state
findingDetails.evidence: Parent and phase-002/003 specs retain obsolete actions/percentages while the phase map and implementation summaries report completion; normal resume precedence contains but does not erase canonical/no-redirect exposure.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Replayed parent pointer/list routing, child implementation-summary precedence, and canonical-doc fallback against all contradictory surfaces.

claimAdjudication.claim: Canonical packet surfaces give obsolete next actions and completion percentages for already-completed research phases.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Child implementation-summary precedence, parent pointer/list routing, null-pointer behavior, and --no-redirect exposure.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Normal child resume and parent child-list fallback contain immediate misrouting, but contradictory canonical metadata remains visible to fallback/no-redirect consumers.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Every completion, indexing, no-redirect, and canonical-doc consumer is proven to suppress the stale spec continuity fields.

findingDetails.title: Hydration has no contracted filesystem-containment boundary
findingDetails.evidence: Hydration re-hashes manifest-selected artifacts but has no canonical-root, traversal, or symlink acceptance criterion or negative fixture.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Owner requirements/tasks/checklist and STUDY consumer were searched for containment controls; only adjacent generation/rights controls exist.

claimAdjudication.claim: A conforming phase-004 implementation can hydrate a path resolving outside the styles root because containment and symlink invariants are unspecified.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Realpath, canonical containment, traversal, symlink, root escape, and outside-root negative fixtures across producer and consumer.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Manifest paths and generation re-hashing may constrain reads in a future implementation, but neither is a binding root-containment invariant.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Canonical containment, explicit symlink policy, immutable safe paths, and negative traversal/escape fixtures become binding.

findingDetails.title: The STUDY injection envelope records a boundary but does not neutralize untrusted instructions
findingDetails.evidence: Corpus observations enter the prompt, while specified leak tests cover copied literals/spans rather than instruction rejection or behavioral non-influence.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Transformer, prompt builder, plan builder, runGuided retry, fixture matrix, and phase-007 authority order were replayed as one producer/consumer path.

claimAdjudication.claim: All planned controls can pass while corpus-borne instructions influence prose because the envelope is metadata and the leak gate tests copied values/spans.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Prompt injection, untrusted instructions, imperative rejection, data-only parsing, sanitization, and behavioral non-influence tests.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: De-literalization and fixed authority may incidentally discard imperatives, but neither requires rejection and literal/span signals miss non-literal influence.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: A closed data-only schema, instruction rejection, and adversarial behavioral non-influence fixtures cover prompt and draft consumers.

findingDetails.title: Canonical parent surfaces omit the implementation-phase dependency graph
findingDetails.evidence: Child docs declare blocking prerequisites, but the parent handoff and every planned child manual.depends_on array omit them; graph indexing maps only depends_on entries into blocks links.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Parent, all phase-004-010 graph/dependency surfaces, child blockers, and the graph blocks consumer were replayed.

claimAdjudication.claim: Canonical parent and graph metadata cannot represent or surface the implementation ordering required by child packets.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Child dependency tables/blocked tasks, parent transitions, every planned-child graph field, and graph consumer semantics.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Child blockers guide humans and limit immediate risk, but graph consumers map only populated depends_on fields into blocks links.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Child-local blockers are proven the sole validated ordering authority and graph blocks are explicitly non-orchestrational/non-retrieval.

findingDetails.title: Two implementation packets classify existing mode and transport surfaces as new creations
findingDetails.evidence: Phase 009 spec/plan and phase 010 spec label existing directory owners Create, while ADR/rollback prose says additions and phase 010 plan says Add.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Action-label search, additive ADR/rollback replay, and current owner reads covered all three surfaces.

claimAdjudication.claim: Owning specs classify three existing runtime surfaces as creations even though planned work is additive, leaving implementation routing contradictory.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Affected-surface matrices, ADR implementation text, rollback semantics, additive requirements, and current owner contracts.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: ADRs and rollback plans strongly contain replacement risk, but owning action labels remain contradictory and additive file boundaries are unnamed.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Dispatch ignores change labels and an authoritative affected-file manifest proves additive-only edits preserving current contracts.

results.summary: Five active P1s survived compact skeptic/referee replay unchanged.
Shared hydration producer lacks root-containment acceptance and negative fixtures.
Literal/span fixtures do not test instruction non-influence.
Resume precedence contains stale continuity; empty dependency metadata suppresses graph blocks links.
Additive intent is clear but owning Create/Add labels conflict.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Five gate-relevant P1s span security boundaries and cross-packet consumers, requiring strict adversarial stabilization replay.

selectedTargets: parent and completed research continuity surfaces
phase-009/010 action labels and current owners
targetSelection.selectionReason: The rendered iteration-005 assignment requires replay of each authoritative P1 with focused counterevidence and consumer impact.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Continuity contradictions may be fully inert under canonical resume precedence.

searchActions.queryOrPath: continuity surfaces plus phase-parent/child resume contracts
searchActions.result: Normal routing contains impact; fallback/no-redirect canonical state remains contradictory.

searchLedger.rationale: Finding confirmed at P1 with narrowed consumer impact.

searchLedger.invariant: Hydration paths must remain canonically contained across every consumer.

searchActions.result: Generation/rights guards exist; root containment and negative escape fixtures do not.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Leak and authority controls may reject instruction influence.

searchActions.queryOrPath: injection rejection and behavioral non-influence criteria
searchActions.result: Only literal/span leak controls and authority ordering exist.

searchLedger.invariant: Blocking packet prerequisites must reach canonical graph consumers.

searchActions.result: Human-readable blockers exist; empty arrays produce no blocks links.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Create labels may unambiguously mean additive child files.

searchActions.queryOrPath: surface tables, ADRs, rollback, current contracts
searchActions.result: Additive intent is clear, but owning Create/Add labels remain contradictory and file boundaries unnamed.

searchLedger.rationale: Classification P1 retained with contained replacement risk.

noveltyJustification: No new or refined finding emerged; focused skeptic/referee replay preserved five P1 severities and resolved only three reducer-generated placeholders.

Graph consumer replay confirmed distinct dependency-projection P1 despite shared parent content hash.

reason: Implementation-summary continuity precedes child spec fallback.

candidate: generation and rights controls prove containment
reason: They do not test traversal, symlinks, or canonical-root escape.

candidate: literal and span gates prove instruction neutrality
reason: Non-literal instruction influence can pass both signals.
Graph blocks are produced only from populated manual.depends_on entries.

candidate: destructive replacement is the stated implementation intent
reason: ADRs and rollback plans consistently state additive work; only classification contradiction remains.
